arguments.query: ClickUp API documentation authentication OAuth API key
ClickUp API pricing free tier developer credentials
ClickUp MCP server model context protocol
clickup.com "API" "api key" generate free account developer
ClickUp API free plan rate limit 100 requests per minute personal token

researched_category.notes: ClickUp is an all-in-one productivity platform, categorized under project management and team collaboration.

one_liner.value: All-in-one productivity platform for managing tasks, docs, goals, chat, and team collaboration.
one_liner.notes: Based on developer portal tagline and product description.

methods.notes: Supports Personal API Token (starts with pk_) for individual use, and OAuth 2.0 Authorization Code flow for apps used by others.

authentication.notes: For an agent toolkit, OAuth 2.0 is the primary recommended method, especially since the official MCP server only supports OAuth. However, personal API tokens are simpler for personal/testing use.

access.notes: Free Forever plan exists with no credit card required. API is available on all plans. Personal API tokens can be self-generated from settings by any user. Some enterprise-only endpoints require Enterprise plan.

summary.value: Comprehensive public REST API (v2 and v3) with OpenAPI specifications covering tasks, lists, folders, spaces, docs, comments, chat, time tracking, custom fields, goals, webhooks, users, guests, and more. Also provides an official MCP server (in public beta) and webhook support.
summary.notes: The llms.txt lists dozens of API reference endpoints. OpenAPI specs available for v2 and v3. MCP server exposes ~36+ tools.

agent_interface.notes: ClickUp has an official MCP server (public beta), community MCP servers on npm/GitHub, and a Composio managed app with 164 tools.

rationale.value: ClickUp has a fully documented public REST API, an official MCP server (with OAuth), a free self-serve tier, and multiple community MCP servers. A developer can sign up for free, generate credentials, and start building an agent toolkit immediately. The API is broad and well-documented. No material blockers exist.
rationale.notes: The only minor friction is that the official MCP server uses OAuth only (not personal API tokens), but this is not a blocker since OAuth is standard for agent integrations.

s1.quote: Build integrations and apps with our Public API. Connect your AI with ClickUp using our MCP Server.
s1.title: ClickUp Developer Portal

s2.quote: Free Forever plan: 60MB Storage, Unlimited Tasks, Unlimited Free Plan Members
s2.title: ClickUp Pricing

s3.quote: Authenticate to the ClickUp API using a personal token or OAuth. For personal use, authenticate with your personal API token. For apps or integrations that other people use, use the OAuth flow
s3.title: Authentication - ClickUp API Docs

s4.quote: Sign up for free and start using ClickUp in seconds. Free Forever. No Credit Card.
s4.title: ClickUp Sign Up

s5.quote: Our Public API is available on all ClickUp Plans. Some endpoints and parameters are limited by plan.
s5.title: API availability by Plan

s6.quote: Free Forever, Unlimited, Business: 100 requests per minute per token.
s6.title: Rate Limits

s7.quote: Lists dozens of API reference endpoints including tasks, folders, lists, spaces, comments, chat, webhooks, custom fields, time tracking, goals, etc.
s7.title: ClickUp Documentation Index (llms.txt)

s8.quote: The raw OpenAPI specification for the ClickUp API is available for each version of our Public API.
s8.title: OpenAPI Specification

s9.quote: Build your own custom integrations and ClickUp apps with our public Application Programming Interface (API).
s9.title: Get Started with the ClickUp API

s10.quote: ClickUp's MCP Server is available on all plans. Our MCP server URL is https://mcp.clickup.com/mcp. We only support OAuth for authentication.
s10.title: ClickUp's MCP Server

s11.quote: The ClickUp MCP exposes many of the capabilities available in our API.
s11.title: MCP Supported Tools

s12.quote: ClickUp ... Auth: OAUTH2, API_KEY. Composio Managed App Available? Yes. Tools: 164.
s12.title: ClickUp - Composio Toolkit

s13.quote: A high-performance Model Context Protocol (MCP) server for integrating ClickUp with AI applications.
s13.title: @sjotie/clickup-mcp - npm
